violations.description: app/core/dependencies.py:419 — `store = getattr(_execution_engine, '_store', None)` accesses the private `_store` attribute of ExecutionEngine. This was acknowledged in the Slice 1 commit body as a known limitation ('independent store construction is N2/Slice 2'). The coupling is fragile: if ExecutionEngine renames or removes `_store` the line silently returns None with no error, making snapshot_writer.set_trade_store() silently a no-op in full-bot mode too.
violations.fix_proposed: Slice 2 must migrate this call: after get_trade_store() is extracted as a DI singleton, replace the getattr block at app/core/dependencies.py:417-421 with `snapshot_writer.set_trade_store(get_trade_store())` (unconditional, no engine-existence guard needed because get_trade_store() constructs the singleton independently). This is option (a) from the cleanup-debt analysis.

cleanup_recommendation: option_a_migrate_getattr_to_singleton — once get_trade_store() is a public DI singleton (Slice 2 deliverable), replacing `getattr(_execution_engine, '_store', None)` at app/core/dependencies.py:419 with a direct `get_trade_store()` call eliminates private-attribute coupling, makes the wiring unconditional (no engine-existence guard needed), and keeps singleton identity across scheduler and engine paths — the exact invariant Slice 2's demo gate must prove (`get_trade_store() is engine.trade_store is scheduler.trade_store`).
